channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump will use drones if cartels don't withdraw
item.description: "If they don't recede, you're gonna see dead bodies all over the place and that's coming soon," O'Reilly says.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk creates confusion about IRS' Direct File
item.description: Billionaire tech mogul Elon Musk posted on his social media site that he has “deleted” 18F, a government agency that worked on technology projects such as the IRS's Direct File program.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein And Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Elon Musk is barreling into government with DOGE, raising unusual legal questions
item.description: The billionaire’s campaign to radically upend federal agencies is stunning former White House officials, even in a political moment when many things are described as unprecedented.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn and Shannon Bond, MPR News

item.title: GOP's Susan Collins to back Tulsi Gabbard confirmation
item.description: Sen. Susan Collins (R-Maine) announced Monday that she will support Tulsi Gabbard to become director of national intelligence (DNI), giving her a key boost in her confirmation push.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: NYC mayor downplays economic concerns of Trump deportation plan
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- There have anecdotal reports that immigration raids are driving undocumented New Yorkers into the shadows. New York City has more than 400,000 undocumented workers who help prop up the economy; however, Mayor Eric Adams said he was not worried they would stop working.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Stacey Abrams-founded voter activist group hit with mass layoffs after record-breaking ethics fine
item.description: A nonprofit political activist group founded by Georgia perennial candidate Stacey Abrams saw major layoffs this weekend after a massive fine was imposed by the state ethics commission.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump targets USAID as Marco Rubio becomes acting head of the embattled agency
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio says he is acting director of the U.S. Agency for International Development, following days of upheaval at USAID as the Trump administration froze many of its programs.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: Multiple Canadian provinces pull US-made booze over Trump tariffs
item.description: At the regional level, provinces are taking their own actions, with multiple premiers saying they have directed their respective liquor boards to stop selling American alcohol.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marco Rubio taking over as acting head of USAID amid agency turmoil
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is being put in charge of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) as the Trump administration mulls the future of the agency.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump defends tariffs as Mexico, Canada and China plan retaliation
item.description: Some of the top imported products from the United States include cars, produce, alcohol, cellphones, clothes and shoes.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Trump used fentanyl to justify tariffs, but the crisis was already easing
item.description: The White House says fentanyl smuggled to the U.S. justifies tariffs against Canada, China and Mexico. But fentanyl deaths and smuggling have been dropping fast — and Canada plays almost no role.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis, Florida GOP tensions spill out into open
item.description: Tensions are spilling out into the open between Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) and members of his own party in the Sunshine State, pointing to a new discordant era of GOP-led politics in a state that has trended red over the last few cycles.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rubio visiting Central America with the Panama Canal and immigration top of mind
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio is on his first foreign trip in office, arriving in Central America on Saturday to press President Donald Trump's top priority — curbing illegal immigration — and to bring the message that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump imposes tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China, raising prospect of higher costs for U.S. consumers
item.description: President Donald Trump on Saturday signed an order to impose stiff tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada and China — fulfilling one of his post-campaign commitments to voters that also carries the risk of sparking higher inflation and disrupting businesses across North America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump fires the director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
item.description: President Donald Trump has fired the director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, Rohit Chopra, in the latest purge of a Biden administration holdover.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: As Trump vows to embrace fossil fuels, U.S. climate policy won't change quickly
item.description: President Trump's first weeks in office included executive orders that aim to stop or roll back former President Biden's ambitious climate agenda and redirect the country's attention to fossil fuels.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Brady, MPR News

item.title: Air traffic controllers union responds to Trump's DEI attacks
item.description: The air traffic controllers union responded Friday to claims by President Trump this week that diversity programs contributed to the fatal crash in Washington Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, Fox 5

item.title: How non-violent undocumented immigrants could be swept up in raids
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – In two weeks of the second Trump administration, the White House is staying true to tough immigration policies with ICE raids across the country. Several have happened in the tri-state area.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Politics Friday: Democrats seek a new leader with Minnesota’s DFL party chair
item.description: MPR News politics editor and host Brian Bakst and his guests talk about the Democratic National Committee’s election of a new party chair and the Minnesotan who’s campaigning for the job. Later, a conversation with newly elected Minnesota Republican Party Chair Alex Plechash.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: More migrants deported under Biden, Baja state official says
item.description: Tijuana Mayor Ismael Burgueño Ruiz says he will not lift an emergency declaration he made last month as a way to prepare for mass deportations from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Trump blames diversity hiring as probe into deadly D.C. plane crash begins
item.description: As the nation reeled from the deadliest American aviation disaster in more than two decades, President Donald Trump on Thursday baselessly blamed diversity initiatives for undermining air safety and questioned the actions of a U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Republicans leave Florida frustrated with lack of final plan to pass Trump agenda
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) arrived at this week’s House Republican retreat with hopes of uniting the fractious GOP conference around a plan to pass President Trump's agenda — but instead, the group is departing south Florida with rising tensions in its ranks. Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis signals veto of TRUMP Act, criticizing lawmakers' immigration bill
item.description: After a late night for lawmakers in Tallahassee, Governor Ron DeSantis says his veto pen is ready to reject the TRUMP Act.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Supreme Court declines to dismiss 'fake electors' appeal
item.description: The Nevada Supreme Court has declined to dismiss an appeal involving the six Republicans charged after falsely claiming President Donald Trump won the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: States lose access to Medicaid payment portal amid Trump federal aid freeze
item.description: State Medicaid programs across the country are reporting they've lost access to federal payment portals one day after President Trump announced a freeze on federal grants and aid.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Ousted IG: Trump firings a 'a potentially existential threat' to government oversight
item.description: One of 17 government watchdogs ousted by President Trump said that the move was a “a potentially existential threat” to a crucial government oversight tool.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right recommendations on health issues
item.description: Fewer than half of Americans in a new poll say they trust President Trump or his health-related nominees to make recommendations on the nation's pressing health care issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order that likely sets in motion a future ban on transgender troops
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order Monday directing Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to revise the Pentagon’s policy on transgender troops, likely setting in motion a future ban on their military service.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
